# Contributor Workflow

## Use this when

- Guiding first-time or external contributors.
- Explaining PR process and review expectations.
- Scoping work into small, reviewable changes.

## Practical guidance

- Prefer small PRs (roughly under 500 lines) when possible.
- Encourage opening draft PRs early for feedback.
- Require tests and docs updates for behavior changes.
- Align proposed work with existing issue context before coding.

## Suggested workflow

1. Confirm issue scope and expected outcome.
2. Write a short implementation plan in the issue/PR.
3. Implement in small commits and open a draft PR early.
4. Run local quality checks and targeted tests.
5. Update docs/release notes when user-visible behavior changes.

## Canonical sources

- `CONTRIBUTING.rst`
- `docs/dev/managing_external_contributions.rst`
- `docs/dev/project_management.rst`
- `docs/dev/data_guidelines.rst`
- `docs/release_notes.rst`
